ci: add type-check script and suppress S1128 for app pages

- Add `type-check` npm script (tsc --noEmit) to frontend/package.json
  to catch type-import regressions like the Phase 8 RequiredFieldsForm/CalendarView incident
- Suppress typescript:S1128 (unused imports) on frontend/app/** in sonar-project.properties

- Remove partition-based URL filtering from CrawlScheduler that silently
  dropped URLs dequeued by the wrong downloader instance
- Remove incompatible flyway-database-postgresql:10.8.1 dependency that
  crashed url-seen (AbstractMethodError with flyway-core 9.x)

to avoid musl libc native library crash, and add --no-watch-fs flag.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Simplify connection path in architecture diagram

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update architecture diagram via draw.io desktop editor

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement distributed web crawler with 6 microservices

Java 21 / Spring Boot 3.2 / Kafka-based pipeline:
- Seed URL Controller: REST API for submitting crawl seeds
- URL Frontier: politeness-enforced, disk-backed URL queue
- HTML Downloader (x3): virtual thread downloads with robots.txt
- Link Extractor (x2): Jsoup-based link parsing
- URL Filter: extension/domain/depth filtering
- URL Seen: Bloom filter + PostgreSQL deduplication

Includes Docker Compose (14 containers), Prometheus/Grafana
monitoring, unit tests, README, and draw.io architecture diagram.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: restore runtime imports stripped by Phase 8 type-import cleanup

RequiredFieldsForm and CalendarView are JSX components, not TypeScript types.
Marking them as `type` imports causes the bundler to strip them at compile
time, resulting in a ReferenceError at render. Removed the `type` keyword.

Dialog with controlled state for confirmation dialogs.

commit.message:
feat(captions): add highlight presets, shadow controls, stroke width, and export parity

- Add Pop-up Highlight (word color), Pop-up Highlight 2 (word box), Single Line 2 (dim/bright) presets
- Implement word-level highlight rendering in preview canvas with per-word timing
- Add shadow controls (color, blur, offset X/Y) to caption style panel
- Add stroke width numeric input, change letter spacing unit from em to px
- Polish caption inputs: custom range thumbs, monospace numbers, no spin buttons
- Improve volume popup styling with backdrop blur, glow thumb, hover states
- Persist volume levels per track type in localStorage
- Apply volume changes immediately during playback respecting ducking state
- Export word-level highlights via per-word drawtext filters in FFmpeg
- Write FFmpeg filter to temp file to avoid Windows command-line length limit
- Apply clean-special-chars to export entries and word timings when enabled
- Set all presets to shadow-based (no stroke) by default, clean-chars checked by default

docs(root_cause_analyzer): add code review, TODOs for Issues F and G

WIKI_REVIEW_ROOT_CAUSE_ANALYZER.md assesses 5 external review comments:
- Issue 1 (cascade double-count): Rejected — cascade and spike timing measure
  independent signals; weight calibration noted as future tuning point
- Issue 2 (fan-out direction): Rejected — penalty correctly models downstream
  followers; mild penalty reduction noted as tuning option
- Issue 3 (error density): Accepted — spike magnitude is a genuine missing signal;
  tracked as Issue F
- Issue 4 (missing containers): Rejected — already handled by fan-out step
- Issue 5 (external node guard): Partially accepted — defensive guard worthwhile;
  tracked as Issue G

Adds TODO comments for Issues F and G to root_cause_analyzer.py.
Updates WIKI_HOME.md Code Reviews table with new entry.

fix: use WSS port 8002 for Samsung TV auth prompt + add docs

for the authorization prompt to appear on screen. Also adds project
documentation covering architecture, protocol details, and setup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add auto_filter_branch config option for TUI (#453)

## Summary

- Adds `auto_filter_branch` global config option
(`~/.roborev/config.toml`) that automatically filters the TUI queue to
the current branch/worktree on launch
- Mirrors the existing `auto_filter_repo` behavior: the filter is added
to the stack (clearable with Escape) but not locked, and `--branch` CLI
flag takes priority
- Adds config round-trip tests (Save/Load and raw TOML parsing) to
verify the TOML tag is correct
- Adds TUI unit tests for auto-filter precedence: standalone, combined
with `auto_filter_repo`, and overridden by `--branch`

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Move connection data to %LOCALAPPDATA%\Span for persistence

MSIX package LocalState is deleted on app uninstall/reinstall,
causing saved FTP/SFTP connections to be lost. Now stores
connections.json and credentials.dat in %LOCALAPPDATA%\Span\
which persists across reinstalls. Includes one-time auto-migration
from the old package folder location.

commit.message:
fix: RISE worker launch cross-platform + auto-reset stuck generations

- Replace exec() with pclose(popen()) for reliable fire-and-forget on Windows/Linux
- Use sys_get_temp_dir() instead of hardcoded /tmp/ for cross-platform log path
- Add Windows-specific start /B command for background process
- Auto-reset generations stuck in "generating" for >5 min before creating new ones

commit.message:
fix: handle non-numeric range values in all exercise formatting helpers

duracion_seg, reps, peso_lb columns can contain range strings like
"20-30" or "2700-3000". Added safe_numeric() helper and applied
pd.to_numeric(errors="coerce") across format_warmup_chip,
format_exercise_compact, and format_exercise_summary to prevent
TypeError on comparisons and format strings.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: eliminate duplicate error output by removing bare top-level await (#292)

Node.js v20+ emits a "Warning: Detected unsettled top-level await" when
the event loop drains before program.parseAsync() resolves — which happens
when Inquirer throws ExitPromptError on prompt cancellation (EOF/SIGINT).
The warning interleaves with the actual error from handleCommandError,
creating apparent duplicate error output.

Fix: replace `await program.parseAsync()` with `.catch()` so no top-level
await is left unsettled at process exit. Commander's own validation errors
(missing required args, unknown commands) call process.exit() internally
and never reach the catch handler. Application errors are already caught
by withCommandContext.

Closes #246

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: suppress stale-run warnings in kata watch for runs missing run.json (#290)

* fix: suppress stale-run warnings in kata watch for runs missing run.json

Runs that predate the run.json feature caused spurious warnings on every
watch refresh. The previous ENOENT code check was ineffective because
JsonStore.read throws JsonStoreError (not a NodeJS.ErrnoException), so
the code property was always undefined and the warn always fired.

Now uses instanceof JsonStoreError + message prefix to distinguish
"file not found" (silent skip) from genuine read/parse errors (still
warn). Adds a regression test for the missing run.json case.

Closes #247

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: apply JsonStoreError check to readStageState (same pattern as readRun)

The readStageState catch block had the same incorrect ENOENT check as
readRun — fixed to use instanceof JsonStoreError to avoid noisy warnings
for missing stage state files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
Update mise docs: prefer shell-level shims over conductor-exec (#2537)

## Summary

- Updated conductor-compatibility docs to reflect that shell-level mise
shim activation (`.zshenv`/`.bashrc`/`.profile` + `BASH_ENV`/`ENV`
exports) is the preferred approach
- Demoted `bin/conductor-exec` wrapper to a fallback for users who
haven't configured shell-level shims
- Added reference to
[justin808-dotfiles](https://github.com/justin808/justin808-dotfiles)
for implementation example
- Added caveat about system binaries that can shadow mise shims on PATH

## Context

With mise shims activated in `.zshenv` (which runs for all zsh shells,
including non-interactive ones used by Conductor and coding agents), the
`conductor-exec` wrapper becomes unnecessary. The `BASH_ENV`/`ENV`
exports ensure child bash/sh processes also get shims.

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify docs render correctly in CLAUDE.md (auto-included via `@`
reference)
- [ ] Confirm conductor-exec still works as fallback

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Low Risk**
> Documentation-only changes that adjust recommended setup and add
guidance; no runtime or build logic is modified.
> 
> **Overview**
> Updates `conductor-compatibility.md` to recommend enabling mise shims
at the shell level (`.zshenv`/`.bashrc`/`.profile` plus
`BASH_ENV`/`ENV`) as the primary fix for non-interactive Conductor/agent
shells using system tool versions.
> 
> Demotes `bin/conductor-exec` to a documented fallback, adds a dotfiles
reference implementation, and notes a PATH-shadowing caveat when system
binaries override mise shims.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor
Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit
0554d9bc1e63ef1a77a30da82f4d26d4e57d590c. Configure
[here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Documentation**
* Recommend shell-level shim activation as the preferred setup for both
interactive and non-interactive shells, with step-by-step per-shell
guidance.
* Clarified environment export behavior, PATH shadowing risks, and
compatibility caveats across shell variants and modes.
* Added extensive user-facing examples and retained a documented
fallback wrapper-based execution option.
